rulings.Case Title: EVA CONDRON-WELLS VS. CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Mtn To Dismiss And Mtn To Quash Service Of Summons; Req For Judicial Notice
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, April 6, 2012, Line 1. DEFENDANTS HINES 101 SECOND STREET LP AND HINES INTEREST LIMITED PARTNERSHIP'S MOtIOn To Dismiss And MOtIOn To Quash Service Of Summons. Granted. Except of the less than one month period (March 23, 2011 through April 20, 2011) from the entry of judgment to the setting aside of that judgment, none of the proffered reasons for tolling the mandatory three year period made it impossible, impracticable or futile for plaintiff to serve the moving parties within three years of plaintiff filing her complaint. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. = (302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: ALBERT SMOTHERS JR VS. CATHOLIC HEALTHCARE WEST DBA SAINT FRANCIS et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, April 6, 2012, Line 6. DEFENDANT CATHOLIC HEALTHCARE WEST DBA SAINT FRANCIS MEMORIAL HOSPITAL'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. Denied. Based on the Mullin and Sockell declarations, there are triable issues whether the care and treatment of the Saint Francis nursing staff fell below the standard of care and was the cause of any damages to the plaintiff. The objections to those declarations are overruled. rulings.Case Title: AHMAD HAKIMJAVADI et al VS. GETTINGE, USA, INC., A CORPORATION et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Tax Costs
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, April 6, 2012, line 19. PLAINTIFF AHMAD HAKIMJAVADI, BEHJAT HAKIMJAVADI Motion To Tax Costs. Granted as to $187 in attachment expenses and $374.20 in models, blowups and photocopies and denied as to all other costs sought to be taxed. Photocopying charges other than for trial exhibits are not recoverable (1033.5(b)(3)). No explanation is provided for the attachment expenses. The deposition and witness costs were reasonably necessary and are recoverable. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. rulings.Case Title: KIN FONG VS. LAWRENCE WONG et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion For Reconsideration Of Order Granting Deft'S Motion To Compel And Award Sanctions Against Pltf In The Sum $2,000
rulings.Rulings: Set for Hearing on Friday, April 6, 2012, Line 2, PLAINTIFF KIN FONG's Motion For Reconsideration Of Order Granting Defendant'S Motion To Compel And Award Sanctions Against Plaintiff In The Sum $2,000 - Pro Tem Judge James Watson, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Department 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem has not issued a tentative ruling on this motion. Each party is required to bring a proposed order to the hearing. =(302/HEK)
